sourceMovie.title: The Blob
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Blob (1988) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chuck Russell's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Blob clicked for you, Resident Evil (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and science fiction, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Blob?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil, Alien: Romulus, The Thing—each shares DNA with The Blob while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Blob?
faq.answer: The Blob (1988) is best described as Horror, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Blob worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
